sections.label: Methods
sections.retrieval_query: study design randomization blinding eligibility primary endpoint statistical methods
sections.drafting_instructions: State: study design, setting, participants (eligibility criteria verbatim from protocol), interventions, primary and secondary outcomes (in order of pre-specification), statistical methods. Use past tense. Do not report results. Copy exact protocol language for eligibility criteria and endpoints. CONSORT and PICO structure. Read like a recipe — every step reproducible. Word limit: 1200.

sections.label: Results
sections.retrieval_query: primary endpoint results p-value confidence interval adverse events
sections.drafting_instructions: Lead with participant flow (screened → randomized → analyzed). Report primary endpoint first: point estimate, 95% CI, p-value. Secondary endpoints in pre-specified order. Safety data last. All numbers must be traceable to a cited source. No interpretation — reporting only. Past tense throughout. Word limit: 1200.

sections.label: Discussion
sections.retrieval_query: clinical significance prior studies limitations generalizability future directions
sections.drafting_instructions: 5 paragraphs: (1) restate primary finding and clinical significance; (2) compare to prior studies with citations; (3) mechanistic rationale; (4) limitations — be explicit and complete; (5) future directions and concluding statement. Present tense for established facts; past tense for this study. Word limit: 1200.

sections.label: Introduction
sections.retrieval_query: disease burden unmet need study rationale prior evidence
sections.drafting_instructions: 3 paragraphs: (1) disease burden and unmet need; (2) prior evidence and remaining gap; (3) this study's objective. Do not report results. Present tense for established facts; past tense for prior studies. Final sentence states the primary endpoint precisely. Word limit: 650.

sections.label: Conclusion
sections.drafting_instructions: 1–3 sentences. Restate the primary finding, its direction, and the clinical implication. No new data. No hedging beyond what the evidence warrants. No references. Word limit: 200.

sections.label: Abstract
sections.retrieval_query: study design primary endpoint results conclusions significance
presets: Summarize per JAMA structured abstract format
sections.drafting_instructions: Structured abstract with labeled sections: Importance, Objective, Design/Setting/Participants, Interventions, Main Outcomes and Measures, Results, Conclusions and Relevance. Past tense throughout. Results: lead with primary endpoint (point estimate, 95% CI, p-value). Do not introduce information not present in the manuscript. Word limit: 300.

sections.label: Plain Language Summary
sections.drafting_instructions: Written for patients and caregivers. US Grade 8–10 reading level. Define all medical terms on first use. Lead with one sentence summarizing the key finding in plain language. Structure: What was the problem? What did researchers do? What did they find? What does it mean for patients? Close with 3–5 Key Takeaways bullets. No citations. No jargon.

sections.label: Citations
sections.drafting_instructions: Annotate every factual claim, statistic, or assertion with a citation marker ([N], [N?], [?], or [!]). Return the annotated draft followed by a numbered Vancouver-style reference list and a summary of all unresolved markers.
